enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with 'unless' or 'if'.

Remember: 'unless' means 'except if' or 'only if not'.

questions.texte: You will pass the exam ______ you study hard.
questions.reponse: You will pass the exam if you study hard.

questions.texte: You will fail the exam ______ you study hard.
questions.reponse: You will fail the exam unless you study hard.

questions.texte: ______ you finish your homework, you can't go out.
questions.reponse: Unless you finish your homework, you can't go out.

questions.texte: She won't go to the party ______ she is invited.
questions.reponse: She won't go to the party unless she is invited.

questions.texte: ______ it rains, we will go to the beach.
questions.reponse: Unless it rains, we will go to the beach.

questions.texte: If you don't apologize, she will be angry.
questions.reponse: Unless you apologize, she will be angry.

questions.texte: If he doesn't arrive soon, we will leave without him.
questions.reponse: Unless he arrives soon, we will leave without him.

questions.texte: I won't buy that car if it is not affordable.
questions.reponse: I won't buy that car unless it is affordable.

questions.texte: You can't enter the club if you don't have an ID.
questions.reponse: You can't enter the club unless you have an ID.

questions.texte: We will be late if we don't hurry.
questions.reponse: We will be late unless we hurry.

enonce.texte:
Rewrite the following sentences using conditional inversions.

Remember: Inversion in conditionals means moving the auxiliary verb to the beginning and dropping 'if'.

Type 2: Were + subject + to + verb... (for hypothetical situations)

Type 3: Had + subject + past participle... (for unreal past situations)

questions.texte: If I were you, I would study harder.
questions.reponse: Were I you, I would study harder.

questions.texte: If he had known the truth, he would have told you.
questions.reponse: Had he known the truth, he would have told you.

questions.texte: If she had studied, she would have passed the exam.
questions.reponse: Had she studied, she would have passed the exam.

questions.texte: If we had seen the sign, we wouldn't have gotten lost.
questions.reponse: Had we seen the sign, we wouldn't have gotten lost.

questions.texte: If I were the president, I would change the education system.
questions.reponse: Were I the president, I would change the education system.

enonce.texte: Read the following situations and complete the sentences using 'unless' or 'if'.

questions.texte: Situation: You want to get a driving license. You need to pass the driving test. - You will get a driving license ______ you pass the test.
questions.reponse: You will get a driving license if you pass the test.

questions.texte: Situation: You want to buy a new phone. You need to save money. - You won't buy a new phone ______ you save enough money.
questions.reponse: You won't buy a new phone unless you save enough money.

questions.texte: Situation: Your friend is very shy and doesn't speak much. You want to know if they will join the school debate club. - My friend will join the debate club ______ she feels more confident.
questions.reponse: My friend will join the debate club unless she feels more confident. (or: if she feels more confident - meaning she will join if she feels confident)

questions.texte: Situation: You need to wake up early tomorrow. Your alarm clock might not work. - I will wake up on time ______ my alarm clock doesn't work.
questions.reponse: I will wake up on time unless my alarm clock doesn't work. (Meaning: I will wake up on time except if my alarm clock doesn't work)

questions.texte: Situation: You want to go on a trip. You need to save money. - I will go on a trip ______ I save enough money.
questions.reponse: I will go on a trip if I save enough money.

enonce.texte: Identify and correct the errors in the following sentences related to 'unless' and conditional inversions.

questions.texte: Unless you will study, you won't pass the exam.
questions.reponse: Unless you study, you won't pass the exam. (The modal 'will' is not used after 'unless'.)

questions.texte: If I were you, I wouldn't had said that.
questions.reponse: If I were you, I wouldn't have said that. (The correct form for unreal past is 'would have' + past participle.)

questions.texte: Had she knew the answer, she would have told us.
questions.reponse: Had she known the answer, she would have told us. (After 'had' in the inversion, we use the past participle 'known', not 'knew'.)

questions.texte: We won't go to the party unless we are not invited.
questions.reponse: We won't go to the party unless we are invited. (The double negative is incorrect. 'Unless' already means 'if not'.)

questions.texte: Were I the principal, I will change the rules.
questions.reponse: Were I the principal, I would change the rules. (The inversion requires 'would' for hypothetical situations, not 'will'.)

enonce.texte: Match the beginning of the sentence with the correct ending.

questions.texte: You will be healthy...
questions.reponse: You will be healthy if you eat well.

questions.texte: You won't be healthy...
questions.reponse: You won't be healthy unless you eat well.

questions.texte: She will forgive you...
questions.reponse: She will forgive you if you apologize.

questions.texte: She won't forgive you...
questions.reponse: She won't forgive you unless you apologize.

questions.texte: I will go to the party...
questions.reponse: I will go to the party if I finish my homework.

enonce.texte:
Transform the following sentences from a formal speech into sentences with conditional inversions.

This is a common technique in formal English to make statements more emphatic or elegant.

questions.texte: If the government had invested more in education, the country would have developed faster.
questions.reponse: Had the government invested more in education, the country would have developed faster.

questions.texte: If we were to improve the infrastructure, the economy would grow.
questions.reponse: Were we to improve the infrastructure, the economy would grow.

questions.texte: If the citizens had participated more, the election would have been more democratic.
questions.reponse: Had the citizens participated more, the election would have been more democratic.

questions.texte: If I were the minister, I would reduce taxes.
questions.reponse: Were I the minister, I would reduce taxes.

questions.texte: If we had known about the crisis earlier, we would have taken action.
questions.reponse: Had we known about the crisis earlier, we would have taken action.

enonce.texte:
Read the following debate topic and complete the arguments using 'unless', 'if', and conditional inversions where appropriate.

Topic: Should students wear uniforms at school?

questions.texte: Complete the argument in favor: 'Students will feel more equal ______ they all wear the same uniform.'
questions.reponse: Students will feel more equal if they all wear the same uniform.

questions.texte: Complete the argument against: 'Students won't express their individuality ______ they are forced to wear uniforms.'
questions.reponse: Students won't express their individuality unless they are forced to wear uniforms. (or: if they are forced to wear uniforms - meaning they won't express themselves if forced)

questions.texte: Complete the argument in favor using a conditional inversion: 'If the school had introduced uniforms earlier, the students would have focused more on their studies.' → ______
questions.reponse: Had the school introduced uniforms earlier, the students would have focused more on their studies.

questions.texte: Complete the argument against using 'unless': '______ schools change this policy, students will continue to complain.'
questions.reponse: Unless schools change this policy, students will continue to complain.

questions.texte: Complete your own argument: 'I believe students should wear uniforms ______ they can focus on their studies without worrying about clothing.'
questions.reponse: I believe students should wear uniforms if they can focus on their studies without worrying about clothing.

enonce.texte:
Write a persuasive paragraph (about 100 words) on the following topic:

Should schools in Cameroon introduce more technology in the classroom?

Use at least two sentences with 'unless', two with 'if', and one conditional inversion.

questions.texte: Write your persuasive paragraph.
questions.reponse: In today's digital age, it is essential for Cameroonian schools to embrace technology. If we integrate tablets and computers into lessons, students will be better prepared for the modern workforce. However, unless the government invests in infrastructure, these efforts will not succeed. Many schools in rural areas lack electricity and internet access. Had we planned better, we could have avoided these challenges. Unless we address these issues, technology will only benefit a few privileged students. If we work together with the private sector, we can find solutions. We should also train our teachers, for they are key to success.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the two sentences with 'unless' and the two with 'if'.
sousQuestions.reponse:
Sentences with 'unless': 1. 'Unless the government invests in infrastructure, these efforts will not succeed.' 2. 'Unless we address these issues, technology will only benefit a few privileged students.'

Sentences with 'if': 1. 'If we integrate tablets and computers into lessons, students will be better prepared for the modern workforce.' 2. 'If we work together with the private sector, we can find solutions.'

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the conditional inversion in your paragraph.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'Had we planned better, we could have avoided these challenges.' (This is a Type 3 conditional inversion showing regret about the past.)

enonce.texte:
Transform the following direct speech sentences into indirect speech, paying attention to the changes in tense, pronouns, and time expressions.

This exercise combines conditionals with reported speech.

questions.texte: He said: 'If you study hard, you will pass the exam.'
questions.reponse: He said that if I studied hard, I would pass the exam. (Type 1 conditional becomes Type 2 in reported speech)

questions.texte: She said: 'Unless you leave now, you will miss the bus.'
questions.reponse: She said that unless I left then, I would miss the bus. (Present becomes past, future becomes conditional)

questions.texte: They said: 'If we had known, we would have helped you.'
questions.reponse: They said that if they had known, they would have helped me. (Type 3 conditional remains unchanged in reported speech)

questions.texte: The teacher said: 'If I were you, I would study more.'
questions.reponse: The teacher said that if she were me, she would study more. (The conditional inversion remains the same)

questions.texte: My friend said: 'Unless I get a scholarship, I won't go to university.'
questions.reponse: My friend said that unless she got a scholarship, she wouldn't go to university. (Tense backshift: get → got, won't → wouldn't)
